introSection: Ragin' Cajun was a Zamperla spinning wild mouse roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Ragin' Cajun was announced on January 19, 2004 along with four other new rides opening at Six Flags Great America for the 2004 season. It opened on May 28, 2004 and was located in a rethemed area of Orleans Place, now known as Mardi Gras.

In July 2013, it was rumored that Ragin' Cajun would be removed from Six Flags Great America after the 2013 season and would be sent to Six Flags America for the 2014 season. The following month, these rumors were confirmed by local sources.

On August 29, 2013, Six Flags America announced the newly rethemed Mardi Gras area for 2014 which included Ragin' Cajun and French Quarter Flyers. The announcement had leaked the day before.

Ragin' Cajun last operated at Six Flags Great America on October 27, 2013 and was quietly removed without any announcement from the park. It was sent to Six Flags America, with its former site remaining vacant until becoming home to The Joker in 2017.

Construction started in February 2014. The roller coaster was erected on the former location of Two Face: The Flip Side, which closed permanently on October 16, 2007 following an accident.

It reopened at Six Flags America on June 21, 2014.

Ragin' Cajun closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

On December 4, 2025, the roller coaster was allegedly listed for sale on amusement-rides.com along with Harley Quinn Spinsanity and SteamWhirler. The listing was removed by December 11.

fullText:
Ragin' Cajun was a Zamperla spinning wild mouse roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA.

Ragin' Cajun was announced on January 19, 2004 along with four other new rides opening at Six Flags Great America for the 2004 season. It opened on May 28, 2004 and was located in a rethemed area of Orleans Place, now known as Mardi Gras.

In July 2013, it was rumored that Ragin' Cajun would be removed from Six Flags Great America after the 2013 season and would be sent to Six Flags America for the 2014 season. The following month, these rumors were confirmed by local sources.

On August 29, 2013, Six Flags America announced the newly rethemed Mardi Gras area for 2014 which included Ragin' Cajun and French Quarter Flyers. The announcement had leaked the day before.

Ragin' Cajun last operated at Six Flags Great America on October 27, 2013 and was quietly removed without any announcement from the park. It was sent to Six Flags America, with its former site remaining vacant until becoming home to The Joker in 2017.

Construction started in February 2014. The roller coaster was erected on the former location of Two Face: The Flip Side, which closed permanently on October 16, 2007 following an accident.

It reopened at Six Flags America on June 21, 2014.

Ragin' Cajun closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

On December 4, 2025, the roller coaster was allegedly listed for sale on amusement-rides.com along with Harley Quinn Spinsanity and SteamWhirler. The listing was removed by December 11.
